topic: Mag-gravity coupling κ = 1.67 nT/µGal — deep-attack on EXP-430 dome-internal-inconsistency surface (P1-P4)
our_argument_summary: WIN-012 retains Self-Contradicted verdict and gains a new third-paragraph attack surfacing the dome's own internal label inconsistency on the κ-numerator event (live-power tile + wins-page + home-page coupling line: BOU 2017 eclipse; home-page Forensic Zoom chart in WIN-070: October 2003 G5 Halloween storm). EXP-430 (P1-P4) settles WIN-012's opening attribution to 2017 eclipse, fixes a residual EXP-401 "BOU 2017 storm" typo, replaces the now-orphaned "single storm event" rebuttal paragraph with a new dome-internal-inconsistency paragraph that references WIN-058b (storm corollary) and WIN-070 (Forensic Zoom chart), and appends a back-pointer from Part 6.14 ("How κ was derived") to WIN-012. ISS-2156 closed. The architecture is now self-consistent on the 2017-eclipse attribution across our review (WIN-012 + Part 6.14 + live-power tile + wins-page).

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No live calculation; full hardcoded identity. Live-power Domain EM-Gravity Coupling tile: Predicted 1.67, Observed 1.677 (= 10.9/6.5), Tolerance 5%, Status PASS. The dome publishes κ = 1.677 from 10.9 nT BOU / 6.5 µGal Mohe per the wins-page; the live-power tile then re-checks that ratio against itself.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Fully hardcoded. The "check" is mathematical identity on the same two numbers that defined κ.
data_sources_used: INTERMAGNET BOU (numerator, now canonically attributed to 2017 North American eclipse, ~92% local coverage per EXP-430 P1)
Wang et al. 2000 / Yang & Wang 2002 (Mohe 1997 denominator, unreplicated; thermal-tilt-artifact alternative per WIN-011)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Five-percent tolerance on a constant defined to equal itself; defender-rescues for 1999/2009 SG nulls (campaigns-not-optimized, Chebychev low-pass, dielectric-boundary-transition reframe) all closed in Part 6.14.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None within WIN-012 detail_evidence post-EXP-430. The cross-page BOU-event inconsistency that ISS-2156 tracked is now settled to 2017 eclipse across all four anchor files (live-power, wins-page, home-page coupling line, WIN-012 opening). The dome's own 2003-storm attribution survives only in the home-page Forensic Zoom chart (per WIN-070), and the new P3 paragraph in WIN-012 surfaces that as a dome-internal inconsistency.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Live-power Domain EM-Gravity Coupling tile (raw-text/08-live-power.txt:224-226): Predicted 1.67, Observed 1.677, Tolerance 5%, Status PASS, Source: BOU 2017 eclipse. The "observed" value 1.677 = 10.9/6.5, recomputing the same hardcoded ratio that defined κ. No live data fetch; no comparison against any independent measurement.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: The dome relabels (i) the Chapman Sq-dynamo ionospheric current explanation of eclipse magnetic anomalies and (ii) the thermal-tilt-artifact / shielding interpretations of the Mohe gravity signal as a unified "magnetic-gravity coupling." The dielectric-boundary-transition reframing of WIN-011 is itself a relabel of contact-phase ionospheric/instrumental effects. No new physical mechanism is derived from disc_radius, firmament_height, or any dome-geometric primitive.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: κ = 1.67 was computed post-hoc from two pre-existing published observations (Mohe 1997, published Wang et al. 2000; BOU 2017 eclipse). Both observations predate the κ definition. No pre-registration.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: κ is a ratio of two independently measured field values; it cannot be derived from disc_radius, firmament_height, or any dome-geometric parameter. EXP-430's new P3 paragraph correctly notes that no first-principles derivation from disc geometry is published.

holes_found.description: P3 paragraph overstates what the Forensic Zoom chart actually labels. The new P3 text reads: "The dome's home-page Forensic Zoom chart labels the same −10.9 nT excursion as the October 2003 G5 Halloween storm at Boulder, 00:55–01:22 UTC, Dst = −383 nT (see WIN-070)." But per WIN-070's own description of the chart (data/wins.json[id=070].detail_evidence, second strong tag), the chart shows a BOU magnetometer ΔH trace with excursions in the ±20.6 to +23.4 nT range — not a labeled −10.9 nT point. The chart is a 1-second-resolution display of the full 27-minute storm window; the dome uses that chart to argue for the same κ = 1.67 nT/µGal constant via a phase-locked r = 0.992 correlation (see WIN-058b), but the chart does not specifically label a "−10.9 nT excursion" as the storm-numerator. Calling it "the same −10.9 nT excursion" reads as if the dome explicitly attributes the κ-numerator value to the 2003 storm — and a dome defender can correctly respond "we never did that; the −10.9 nT figure is unambiguously the 2017 eclipse per the live-power tile and wins-page; the 2003 chart is a separate validation via correlation." The structural attack still works (the dome reuses the same κ across two physically distinct regimes without a first-principles derivation from disc geometry), but the specific labeling claim invites a precision-based defender rebuttal that weakens P3 rhetorically.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten the P3 wording. Suggested replacement for the second and third sentences of P3: "The dome's home-page Forensic Zoom chart uses BOU magnetometer data from the October 2003 G5 Halloween storm (00:55–01:22 UTC, Dst = −383 nT; see WIN-070) to argue for the same κ = 1.67 nT/µGal constant via a phase-locked r = 0.992 correlation (Exhibit A; see also WIN-058b). The dome thereby applies κ across two physically distinct event types — a 2017 ionospheric eclipse perturbation (the κ-numerator) and a 2003 ring-current storm (the chart's validation context) — without publishing any first-principles derivation from disc geometry showing why a single coupling constant should hold across both regimes." This preserves the dome-internal-inconsistency attack (same κ, two regimes, no derivation) while removing the imprecise "labels the same −10.9 nT excursion" claim that a defender can correctly contest.

holes_found.description: The `finding` field still lags `detail_evidence` after EXP-430. Current finding: "WIN-012 claims 6.5 µGal eclipse gravity anomaly to derive κ = 1.67, but WIN-013/014 report 0.0 µGal — denominator vanishes, κ undefined. Coupling constant derived circularly from unconfirmed Mohe data; monitor.py validates via hardcoded identity check." This is the c1-era surface-contradiction framing; it does not surface the new dome-internal-labels inconsistency (which is now the centerpiece attack in detail_evidence), the κ-as-bridge structural vise (raised in c7 stronger_arguments item #2, never integrated), or the post-EXP-430 BOU 2017 eclipse attribution. Most readers see the `finding` line in the summary table before deciding whether to expand the detail. Leaving it stale degrades the entry-point and means the dome-internal-labels argument — the rhetorically strongest move per c7 — is invisible until expand. c7 stronger_arguments item #2 explicitly called for this; EXP-430 scope was detail_evidence + Part 6.14 only and did not touch `finding`.
holes_found.recommendation: Update `finding` to reflect the post-EXP-430 argument. Suggested rewrite: "WIN-012 derives κ = 1.67 nT/µGal from −10.9 nT (BOU 2017 eclipse) ÷ −6.5 µGal (Mohe 1997 eclipse), but WIN-013/014 report 0.0 µGal at later eclipses — denominator vanishes, κ undefined. The dome itself applies the same κ value across two physically distinct event types (2017 eclipse, 2003 G5 storm; see WIN-058b, WIN-070) without publishing any derivation from disc geometry that would make the constant universal across regimes." This carries the structural vise + the dome-internal-labels inconsistency in the table-row summary, matching detail_evidence.

holes_found.description: P3 paragraph's phrase "Absent such a derivation, the numerical agreement is parameter-fit residue, not a confirmed universal constant" is structurally ambiguous. There is no independent storm-derived value of 1.677 to be in "numerical agreement" with the eclipse-derived 1.677 — the dome ASSERTS κ = 1.677 and uses the storm event to show an r = 0.992 cross-correlation, not to re-derive κ. A defender can respond: "We never claimed two independent derivations agreeing on 1.677; we have one definition (eclipse) and one consistency check (storm via correlation)." The attack still works because consistency without derivation is still parameter-fit, but the "numerical agreement" framing invites the response. Closely related to Hole 1 (both stem from imprecision about what the dome's two κ-claims actually are).
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: replace "the numerical agreement is parameter-fit residue" with "the dome's claim of κ universality across regimes is parameter-fit residue" or "the reuse of κ across distinct regimes is unanchored to dome geometry." Lower-priority than Hole 1; can be folded into the same edit.

holes_found.description: WIN-070 cross-reference now appears twice in WIN-012 detail_evidence: once in the new P3 paragraph ("... at Boulder, 00:55–01:22 UTC, Dst = −383 nT (see WIN-070)") and once in the final Cross-references paragraph ("For the dome's home-page Forensic Zoom chart claiming to show κ via GRACE L1A residuals (BOU magnetic + L1A induction r = 0.992, AI = 1.152), see WIN-070"). Not a defect — the two contexts are distinct (P3 cites WIN-070 for the chart's 2003-storm attribution; Cross-references cites WIN-070 for the GRACE L1A graph-forensics critique) — but a stylistic redundancy. Could be deduplicated by removing one instance, or differentiated by inline phrasing (e.g., "WIN-070's graph-forensics critique" in Cross-references).
holes_found.recommendation: Optional. Low priority. If Hole 1 is patched by rewriting P3, the duplicate link can naturally be cleaned up at the same time.

stronger_arguments.description: The dome's claim type "two independent eclipse events, same coupling constant" (raw-text/02-wins.txt:87) is itself a self-contradiction with WIN-058b's storm corollary. The dome already applies the same κ value to three distinct events: (a) 2017 BOU eclipse (numerator), (b) 1997 Mohe eclipse (denominator), (c) 2003 BOU storm (Forensic Zoom validation, WIN-058b r = 0.992). That is THREE events using one constant, not "two independent eclipse events" — and one of the three (the 2003 storm) is not an eclipse at all. The dome's own framing therefore double-counts: the wins-page claims "two eclipse events" while the home-page chart and WIN-058b add a third storm event under the same κ. Our P3 paragraph touches this ("WIN-058b ... is the dome's attempt to live in both regimes") but does not name the count contradiction (2 vs 3) explicitly. Naming the count makes the dome's self-contradiction crisper.
sources: raw-text/02-wins.txt:87 ("Two independent eclipse events, same coupling constant")
data/wins.json[id=070].detail_claim (Forensic Zoom chart using 2003 storm BOU data to argue for the same κ)

stronger_arguments.description: Promote the κ-as-bridge structural vise from Part 6.14 to WIN-012's `finding` field — same recommendation as c7 stronger_arguments item #2, still not integrated by EXP-430 (which intentionally scoped to detail_evidence + Part 6.14). Either κ applies (in which case 1999/2009 SG nulls refute the model) or κ has unpublished post-hoc scope conditions (in which case PRED-TIER-3 has no derivation). Surfacing this in the summary-table row would close the c7 ask and make the load-bearing argument visible without an expand-click. See Hole 2 for the specific finding-text proposal.

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-430 is a real architectural improvement and lands the c7 "option (a, preferred)" closure of ISS-2156 cleanly. Settling WIN-012's opening BOU attribution to the 2017 eclipse aligns our review with the dome's own live-power tile (raw-text/08-live-power.txt:226 "Source: BOU 2017 eclipse"), the wins-page (raw-text/02-wins.txt:87 "BOU 2017 + Mohe 1997"), and the home-page coupling line (raw-text/01-home.txt:32). The new P3 paragraph in WIN-012 (data/wins.json[id=012].detail_evidence) implements the c7 "rhetorically strongest move" of surfacing the dome's own internal labeling inconsistency between live-power tile and Forensic Zoom chart, with WIN-058b and WIN-070 cross-references. The Part 6.14 back-pointer (data/sections.json:part6.html, P4) closes the symmetric side. Verdict (Self-Contradicted) is preserved and is now better-supported because the surface-contradiction argument (WIN-013/014 nulls) is joined by a dome-internal-labels argument that the dome cannot rebut by reframing WIN-013/014.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The EXP closes ISS-2156 but the executed P3 paragraph overstates the dome's actual labeling claim — the Forensic Zoom chart does not specifically label "the same −10.9 nT excursion" as the 2003 storm (per data/wins.json[id=070].detail_evidence the chart shows ±20.6 to +23.4 nT excursions). The chart instead asserts that the storm data validates the same κ value via r = 0.992 correlation. Hole 1 in this review (moderate) flags the precision issue; a follow-up minor_edit can tighten the P3 wording without losing the structural inconsistency-surface attack. Separately, the `finding` field (data/wins.json[id=012].finding) remains stale relative to the new detail_evidence centerpiece — Hole 2 in this review (moderate) recommends updating it to surface the dome-internal-labels argument and the κ-as-bridge vise to readers who never expand the entry.

deeper_analysis:
Post-EXP-430 the WIN-012 entry-point now has a three-paragraph attack structure: (Para 1) the contradiction stated up front — κ definition + the killer move (WIN-013/014 nulls void the denominator); (Para 2) three-step refutation with Unnikrishnan, Van Camp, Sun citations; (Para 3, NEW) the dome's own labels of the same number disagree — live-power tile vs Forensic Zoom chart, with WIN-058b storm-corollary pre-emption. This is a structural improvement on the c8 architecture (which had the surface-contradiction attack only; the dome-internal-labels argument lived in c7 stronger_arguments item #1 but never made it into the prose). Part 6.14 (sections.json:part6.html) now back-references WIN-012 at the natural cross-link point (just after the κ definition paragraph), so a reader who reaches Part 6.14 via the kappa-cluster TLDR is routed back to WIN-012 for the inconsistency-surface argument and forward to the rest of Part 6.14 for the empirical-test closure (1999/2009 SG nulls falsify κ at the eclipses it predicts to apply to).

The two moderate holes in this review (P3 overstating the chart's labeling; `finding` field still lagging detail_evidence) are both addressable by a single small follow-up EXP. Neither is structural — they are precision and entry-point-visibility issues respectively. The verdict (Self-Contradicted) is preserved with confidence ~0.88 (lower than c7's 0.92 because Hole 1 introduces a defender-rebuttable precision issue in the new prose; once Hole 1 is patched, confidence should return to ~0.92).

Considering the other side (per user preference): a fair-minded dome defender will read the new P3 and respond "You're mischaracterizing our chart. The Forensic Zoom chart is Exhibit A for the same κ that's defined in WIN-012 via the 2017 eclipse, and it shows the storm-context r = 0.992 correlation. We are not claiming the chart's data RE-DERIVES κ from the storm; we are claiming the storm data is consistent with the same κ. Your attack reduces to 'the dome reuses κ across regimes without first-principles derivation,' which is a real critique but doesn't require the false 'labels the same −10.9 nT excursion' framing." This defender response would be correct on the precision point; tightening P3 per Hole 1's recommendation removes the rebuttable wording while preserving the structural attack.

Meta-observation for tinker: the c7-to-EXP-430 cycle worked as designed. c7 identified the rhetorically strongest move (stronger_arguments item #1 surfacing dome-internal labels); the decider routed it to the analyst (correctly, because it required deciding canonical attribution and writing new prose, not a sed-patch); the analyst wrote EXP-430 with a clear scope (P1-P4), explicit anchor-uniqueness verification, anticipated_objections, and a stated kernel_of_truth. The integration landed all four patches correctly. The two holes flagged here are precision issues that incremental EXPs are expected to surface — this is the expected curmudgeon-on-EXP-integration role under PROP-025 deep-attack classification, and the precision issues are exactly the kind of finding that justifies routing this item as `class: deep-attack` (singleton) rather than `class: verification` (batchable). Gate working as designed.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender reads the post-EXP-430 WIN-012 and constructs the following rebuttal: "Your P3 paragraph claims our Forensic Zoom chart labels the same −10.9 nT excursion as the 2003 storm. That is factually wrong — our chart shows BOU storm magnitudes around ±20-23 nT (per your own WIN-070 verification), not −10.9 nT. We never claimed the storm produces the −10.9 nT numerator that defines κ; the chart shows the storm-context r = 0.992 correlation as Exhibit A for the same κ value already defined by the 2017 eclipse. Your 'If the numerator is the 2003 storm, then κ is a storm-magnetic / eclipse-gravity ratio' hypothetical is attacking a position we don't hold." The defender pushes further: "Your real critique reduces to 'the dome reuses κ across regimes without first-principles derivation,' which is fair, but you don't need the labeling-inconsistency framing for that — and the labeling framing weakens your argument by inviting precision rebuttals."
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Patching Hole 1 (tighten P3 to remove "labels the same −10.9 nT excursion" and replace with "uses BOU magnetometer data from the storm to argue for the same κ via r = 0.992") removes the precision-rebuttable surface while preserving the structural attack (same κ, two regimes, no derivation from disc geometry). Patching Hole 2 (update `finding` field) raises the κ-as-bridge structural vise to the entry-point. After both patches, defense_survives drops to ~0.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: Post-EXP-430, the BOU-event-attribution conflict between WIN-012 and Part 6.14 is settled (both now say 2017 eclipse). The remaining cross-WIN tension is that WIN-058b's storm-context κ-validation and WIN-070's Forensic Zoom chart description both reference the 2003 G5 Halloween storm context for the same κ value as WIN-012's eclipse-derived definition. This is the dome-internal inconsistency our P3 paragraph attacks (correctly in structural terms, imprecisely in specific wording — see Hole 1). The dome's own raw-text/02-wins.txt:87 claims "two independent eclipse events, same coupling constant," but WIN-058b shows the dome already extends κ to a third event (the 2003 storm) — see stronger_arguments item #1 for the explicit count contradiction (2 vs 3).

quantitative_verification.computations: P3 length: 1536 chars (vs EXP-430 projection ~561 delta from base 4339, actual delta from base 5734-4339 = 1395, of which P3 paragraph is the dominant contribution net of the removed "single storm event" paragraph that was ~327 chars per c7 review). All cited dome raw-text line numbers exact-match. κ arithmetic unchanged from c7/c8 (10.9/6.5 = 1.6769, rounds to 1.677 at 3dp, 1.68 at 2dp; dome quotes 1.67 — pre-existing 1-significant-figure rounding choice, not flagged).
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: One precision discrepancy in P3 (the chart's actual labeled magnitudes vs the "same −10.9 nT excursion" wording) — see Hole 1.

summary_for_decider: WIN-012 c9 deep-attack review on EXP-430 (queue_id 394). EXP-430 integration is structurally correct: P1-P4 land cleanly, ISS-2156 closed, BOU 2017 eclipse attribution now consistent across our review and the dome's own live-power/wins-page/home-page. Verdict (Self-Contradicted) holds. TWO MODERATE HOLES flagged: (H1) P3 paragraph overstates the dome's Forensic Zoom chart by claiming it "labels the same −10.9 nT excursion as the 2003 storm" — per WIN-070, the chart shows ±20-23 nT excursions, not a labeled −10.9 nT point. The chart asserts the same κ value, not the same magnetic magnitude. Recommend tightening P3 wording (see recommendation). (H2) `finding` field still lags detail_evidence — the c7 stronger_arguments #2 ask to promote κ-as-bridge vise to summary table remains unfulfilled. Two minor holes: P3 "numerical agreement" framing ambiguity (folds into H1 patch); duplicate WIN-070 cross-reference (cosmetic). Recommended action: minor_edit — close H1 and H2 in a small follow-up. NO verdict change; class correctly set to deep-attack (the precision and entry-point issues are exactly the kind of finding that justifies deep-attack singleton scope). Note: confidence dropped from c8/0.9 to c9/0.88 because H1 introduces a defender-rebuttable surface in the new prose; patching H1 should restore to ~0.92.
